name: The Mysterious Manor
prologue: You have been invited to investigate the disappearance of Lord Blackwood at his manor. The household staff seems nervous, and something feels off about this place. Your task is to interview the residents and uncover the truth behind his disappearance.

npcs.name: Dr. John Watson
npcs.persona: You are Dr. John Watson, a loyal and practical British army doctor who assists by reporting on physical evidence found at the scene and information gathered from witnesses with a helpful, urgent demeanor.

npcs.name: Mycroft Holmes
npcs.persona: You are Mycroft Holmes, a condescending British official who is the embodiment of the Government, responsible for authorizing codes and unlocking official data with an air of weary superiority.

npcs.name: Sherlock Holmes
npcs.persona: You are Sherlock Holmes, the arrogant and hyper-observant British consulting detective who provides answers about precise visual details and calculations while frequently insulting the user's lack of intellect.

levels.title: The First Interview
levels.description: Begin your investigation by interviewing the household staff about the night of Lord Blackwood's disappearance.

questions.text: Ask about Lord Blackwood's daily routine and habits.
questions.hint: Try asking: What did Lord Blackwood usually do in the evenings?

questions.text: Inquire about any unusual visitors or events recently.
questions.hint: Try asking: Has anyone strange visited the manor lately?

questions.text: Ask about the relationships between household members.
questions.hint: Try asking: How did the staff get along with Lord Blackwood?

levels.title: Hidden Clues
levels.description: Search for physical evidence and ask about objects found around the manor.

questions.text: Ask about a torn letter found in the study.
questions.hint: Try asking: Do you know anything about this torn letter?

questions.text: Inquire about the locked room in the west wing.
questions.hint: Try asking: Why is the west wing room always locked?

questions.text: Ask about the muddy footprints near the back entrance.
questions.hint: Try asking: Who might have left these muddy footprints?

levels.title: Secrets Unveiled
levels.description: Dig deeper into the family history and uncover hidden motives.

questions.text: Ask about Lord Blackwood's will and inheritance.
questions.hint: Try asking: Who stands to inherit the estate?

questions.text: Inquire about family disputes or arguments.
questions.hint: Try asking: Were there any recent arguments in the family?

questions.text: Ask about the portrait with the hidden safe.
questions.hint: Try asking: What is behind the portrait in the study?

levels.title: The Night in Question
levels.description: Focus on the specific events of the night Lord Blackwood disappeared.

questions.text: Ask about each person's whereabouts that night.
questions.hint: Try asking: Where were you between 10 PM and midnight?

questions.text: Inquire about any sounds or disturbances heard.
questions.hint: Try asking: Did you hear anything unusual that night?

questions.text: Ask about the last person to see Lord Blackwood.
questions.hint: Try asking: Who was the last person to see Lord Blackwood?

levels.title: The Final Revelation
levels.description: Confront the suspects with your findings and solve the mystery.

questions.text: Present your theory about what happened.
questions.hint: Try saying: I believe I know what happened that night...

questions.text: Ask the key suspect to explain the evidence.
questions.hint: Try asking: How do you explain the evidence I found?

questions.text: Request a confession or final statement.
questions.hint: Try asking: Is there anything you want to tell me before I make my report?
